key: my daughters did not go to school for a year and a half because of blue state pandemic school closure policies. the whole country is now paying the price for democratic political cowardice.

work: A Pro-Ed-Reform President Would Cure the Blue-State Blues

key: in red states, you get free money for the school of your choice. in blue states, you get what you get, and you do not get upset. voters now face a clear color-coded national split screen.
quote: In red states, you get free money for the school of your choice. In blue states, you get what you get, and you do not get upset. Voters now face a clear color-coded national split screen.

key: for decades, progressive education reformers like weingarten have argued that schools spend too much time on facts and 'rote' memorization and not enough on projects, experiences, and real-world applications.

work: In the Age of AI Knowledge Matters More Not Less

key: these kids in the tiktok video aren't the victims of covid and school closures, or brain-dead illiterate social media addicts... they are ken goodman's kids.

key: even the inconsistent in-person school is still better than learning remotely on screen.

key: children are uncivilized little beasts and they have to be whipped into shape. their every impulse must be tightly controlled, their behavior constantly monitored, and compliance regularly enforced.
quote: Children are uncivilized little beasts and they have to be whipped into shape. Their every impulse must be tightly controlled, their behavior constantly monitored, and compliance regularly enforced.

key: there are many ways to divide up the sides of the education debates. one is between those who believe in civic democratic ideals and those who do not.

work: Undemocratic School Primer

key: premise 1: all human beings have equal worth and are equally deserving of autonomy, opportunity, power, and privilege. under this premise, the school system would exist to provide opportunity to all students.
quote: Premise 1: All human beings have equal worth and are equally deserving of autonomy, opportunity, power, and privilege. Under this premise, the school system would exist to provide opportunity to all students.

work: Undemocratic School Primer

key: what's that big state at the bottom with 40 electoral votes? -- a 7th-grade student referring to texas, which prompted the author's entire approach.

key: the timelines certainly seem to match up. england made big reforms to its education system, and mental health and social problems spiked soon after. but of course, correlation isn't causation.

work: Do Knowledge-Rich Curriculums Cause Mental Health Problems

key: the promise of models like alpha is not that ai can replace teachers or compress academics into two hours. the promise is that technology can free up time for experiences that traditional schools have struggled to prioritize.
quote: The promise of models like Alpha is not that AI can replace teachers or compress academics into two hours. The promise is that technology can free up time for experiences that traditional schools have struggled to prioritize.
author: Evolving Education
work: If We Redesign School What Should We Build Instead

key: most ai literacy curricula begin and end with the same lesson: here's how to use chatgpt responsibly. learn to prompt. check your sources. don't plagiarize. that's not ai literacy. that's a typing tutorial for 2023.
quote: Most AI literacy curricula begin and end with the same lesson: here's how to use ChatGPT responsibly. Learn to prompt. Check your sources. Don't plagiarize. That's not AI literacy. That's a typing tutorial for 2023.

key: the ai crisis in education is not that students suddenly learned to outsource their thinking.
quote: The AI crisis in education is not that students suddenly learned to outsource their thinking.

key: when ai can produce polished written work on demand, the ability to write an essay becomes less distinctive. but ai can't participate in a live socratic dialogue.

work: Where Is Education Heading Four Forces Reshaping K12

key: ai tools are amplifiers of human input. the quality of what ai produces depends heavily on the skills, knowledge, and judgment of the person using it.
quote: AI tools are amplifiers of human input. The quality of what AI produces depends heavily on the skills, knowledge, and judgment of the person using it.

work: Federal Government AI Literacy Framework Teaching AI with Complementary Human Skills

key: post-graduate communication outside academia will generally involve speaking and debating. we need a rebalance toward orality.

key: every person will spend far more time speaking, presenting, and verbally defending ideas than they will writing formal essays. job interviews are spoken. team meetings are spoken.
quote: Every person will spend far more time speaking, presenting, and verbally defending ideas than they will writing formal essays. Job interviews are spoken. Team meetings are spoken.
author: Bauschard, Stefan
work: Bauschard's Initial Guide to Classroom Debate Implementation and Assessment

key: 58% of secondary teachers said they had suspected a student of using ai in the past month, even though they had been told not to. that number is striking -- but i think it is a significant underestimate.

key: nuance is an overused word to describe someone's views... but i don't know of a better one to describe dan's writing on edtech. here, he argues that the call to ban (or reduce) screens in schools is too blunt.

key: for novice teachers, that mentoring relationship is irreplaceable. ai can be a useful supplement, but it should not be the dominant resource they turn to. if we have a problem with access to mentors, we fix the mentoring problem.
quote: For novice teachers, that mentoring relationship is irreplaceable. AI can be a useful supplement, but it should not be the dominant resource they turn to. If we have a problem with access to mentors, we fix the mentoring problem.

key: that is trap number one: cognitive outsourcing, to use paul kirschner's phrase. the ai does the thinking, you copy and paste, and your own pedagogical reasoning slowly atrophies.
quote: That is trap number one: cognitive outsourcing, to use Paul Kirschner's phrase. The AI does the thinking, you copy and paste, and your own pedagogical reasoning slowly atrophies.

key: outsourcing -- asking ai to explain, summarise, write, or produce -- transfers the thinking itself to an external agent. the student receives a finished product. nothing is encoded in long-term memory.
quote: Outsourcing -- asking AI to explain, summarise, write, or produce -- transfers the thinking itself to an external agent. The student receives a finished product. Nothing is encoded in long-term memory.
author: Jamie Lee Clark
work: The Generation Effect

key: the future of education is not about getting through content faster. information has already become abundant and accessible. the real challenge is helping young people discover who they are, how they contribute, how they work with others, and how they navigate an unpredictable world.
quote: The future of education is not about getting through content faster. Information has already become abundant and accessible. The real challenge is helping young people discover who they are, how they contribute, how they work with others, and how they navigate an unpredictable world.

key: what needs to change is not the importance of learning. what needs to change is what and how we learn, and the design of the ecosystem surrounding it.

key: when we summoned them back, we did not really account for what had just happened. we didn't own the contradiction. we said, 'no, no, you have to come back to the classroom. learning is better face-to-face.' there is a falseness there that higher education hasn't owned up to.
quote: When we summoned them back, we did not really account for what had just happened. We didn't own the contradiction. We said, 'No, no, you have to come back to the classroom. Learning is better face-to-face.' There is a falseness there that higher education hasn't owned up to.

key: ai has extended that same dynamic. now the apathy is not just about the classroom; it is about what we ask students to do. students are looking at us, looking at these tools, looking at the world they are being told they are preparing for, and asking: really?
quote: AI has extended that same dynamic. Now the apathy is not just about the classroom; it is about what we ask students to do. Students are looking at us, looking at these tools, looking at the world they are being told they are preparing for, and asking: really?

key: i do not think the answer is that the work does not matter. reading matters. writing matters. research matters. thinking carefully and making meaning across complexity absolutely matters. but we need a better reason than a grade.
quote: I do not think the answer is that the work does not matter. Reading matters. Writing matters. Research matters. Thinking carefully and making meaning across complexity absolutely matters. But we need a better reason than a grade.

key: the ruling contradiction is that education is structured around efficiency and convenience to the institution's benefit -- and moments like the pandemic and now ai make that glaringly evident.

key: every time you let ai do your thinking, you're not cheating the institution, you're cheating yourself out of the cognitive development that actually makes you capable. you can't build a school culture of integrity from 8 to 3 and then send students home into an ecosystem engineered to erode their agency.
quote: Every time you let AI do your thinking, you're not cheating the institution, you're cheating yourself out of the cognitive development that actually makes you capable. You can't build a school culture of integrity from 8 to 3 and then send students home into an ecosystem engineered to erode their agency.

key: we don't tell them they're learning ai. we tell them they have a problem and we have a tool. the problem has to be real -- something they actually care about, something that affects them or someone they love. the tool is google ai studio. the rest is theirs.

key: one student built a multiplication tutor for her younger brother in about three hours. she had never programmed before. she had never heard of an api. she had a brother who struggled with math and a three-hour block of time and a very clear sense of what she wanted it to do. that was enough.
quote: One student built a multiplication tutor for her younger brother in about three hours. She had never programmed before. She had never heard of an API. She had a brother who struggled with math and a three-hour block of time and a very clear sense of what she wanted it to do. That was enough.

work: Unleashing Kid Wizards

key: the cheating frame is the enemy of real ai education. when you teach kids that ai is the thing you use instead of doing the work, you've taught them something false and damaging. ai is a tool for doing harder work than you could do without it. the camp is designed around that premise.
quote: The cheating frame is the enemy of real AI education. When you teach kids that AI is the thing you use instead of doing the work, you've taught them something false and damaging. AI is a tool for doing harder work than you could do without it. The camp is designed around that premise.
author: Stefan Bauschard
work: Unleashing Kid Wizards

key: what we're watching is a generation of kids develop a fundamentally different relationship to problem-solving. they don't ask 'can i do this?' they ask 'what tool do i need?' that shift in the framing of capability is what we're trying to produce, and we're watching it happen.
quote: What we're watching is a generation of kids develop a fundamentally different relationship to problem-solving. They don't ask 'can I do this?' They ask 'what tool do I need?' That shift in the framing of capability is what we're trying to produce, and we're watching it happen.
author: Stefan Bauschard
work: Unleashing Kid Wizards
